observed_behavior: After fixes, hosted health reported priceWatcher.connected=true with cacheSize=452, final quick smoke received data in 0.68s, and combined 50.2s smoke stayed open past the old close window with no close while receiving all-watch=1257, all-firehose=1257, poly-watch=805 data frames.
notes: Root causes were Cloud Run 30s request timeout, stale hosted obdata route, and stale hosted obdata relay key. Protocol ping alone did not fix production; app heartbeat proved frames were visible but timeout still closed at ~32s. Cloud Run --timeout=900s fixed connection lifetime; OBDATA_WS_URL pin plus obdata WS_API_KEYS rotation restored upstream book data.

user_story: As a hosted trader, I want to submit a signed built order so that the trade is placed after explicit wallet approval.

test_command: npm test --workspace=pmxtjs -- --runInBand tests/hosted-dispatch.test.ts; python3 -m pytest sdks/python/tests/test_hosted_dispatch.py; pmxt-trading uv run --extra dev pytest services/trading-api/tests/test_compute_quote_key.py services/trading-api/tests/test_v0_endpoints.py -q; live authenticated non-mutating POST /v0/trade/build-order then invalid-signature POST /v0/trade/submit-order smoke using PMXT_API_KEY and wallet address
observed_behavior: SDK dispatch tests verify submitOrder routes to POST /v0/trade/submit-order with signer flow and raises locally when signer/signature requirements are missing. Live smoke built a valid order for will-congo-dr-win-the-2026-fifa-world-cup with built_order_id and typed_data. Before fix, invalid signature submit returned HTTP 500 with no structured body. After pmxt-trading fix and Hetzner deploy, the same invalid-signature path returned HTTP 422 with detail.code=invalid_signature and detail.message=Invalid signature; open order count stayed 1 before and after.
notes: Valid live signed submit remains intentionally unattempted because it would place an order and spend funds; negative validation path and no-mutation behavior verified live.

notes: Live authenticated createOrder orchestration reached production build-sign-submit safely via mismatched signer. Valid live signed createOrder remains intentionally unattempted because it would place an order and spend funds.

user_story: As a TypeScript developer, I want a typed PMXT client so that I can call local or hosted PMXT methods from Node applications.
expected_behavior: The pmxtjs package exposes exchange classes with camelCase methods, converts response models, chooses hosted or local base URL, and dispatches hosted trading when PMXT API key and wallet configuration require it.
test_command: npm test --workspace=pmxtjs -- --runInBand tests/hosted-dispatch.test.ts; python3 -m pytest sdks/python/tests/test_hosted_dispatch.py; npx tsc --project sdks/typescript/tsconfig.json --noEmit; npm test --workspace=pmxtjs; RED/GREEN: npm test --workspace=pmxtjs -- --runInBand sdks/typescript/tests/sidecar-order-param-forwarding.test.ts; npm test --workspace=pmxtjs -- --runInBand
observed_behavior: Retest caught three TypeScript SDK regressions: hosted v0 read methods did not consistently unwrap {balances|positions|order} envelopes, explicit local baseUrl clients still followed a sidecar lock on a different port, and PMXT_BASE_URL=LOCAL_URL clients also followed that lock. After fixes, focused hosted dispatch passed 18 tests, Python parity passed 26 tests, TypeScript typecheck passed, and the full TypeScript SDK suite passed 53 tests.
error_id: TS-SDK-001; TS-SDK-002; TS-SDK-003; TS-SDK-V0-WRAPPER-001; TS-SDK-BASEURL-001; TS-SDK-BASEURL-002
notes: TypeScript SDK logistical failures fixed and fully retested, including hosted v0 wrapper parsing, explicit local baseUrl precedence, and PMXT_BASE_URL env override precedence over sidecar lock discovery.

user_story: As a Python developer, I want a Pythonic PMXT client so that I can call the same PMXT features with snake_case methods.

test_command: python3 -m pytest sdks/python/tests; python3 -c 'import sys; sys.path.insert(0, "sdks/python"); import pmxt; h=pmxt.Hunch(); print(type(h).__name__, getattr(h, "exchange_name", None))'
observed_behavior: Python SDK test suite previously passed 263 selected tests. Current smoke initially reproduced PMXT server startup timeout through the default local constructor path; after ac31338, pmxt.Hunch() constructed successfully against the real local sidecar and printed Hunch hunch.
notes: Default Python exchange construction now survives an unhealthy existing launcher lock after the shared launcher refreshes it.

user_story: As an SDK or CLI user, I want to start, stop, restart, and check a local PMXT service so that self-hosted calls work without manual server management.
expected_behavior: Server manager helpers and launchers locate or launch the bundled local service, refresh unhealthy stale locks, expose status, start, stop, restart, health, and logs behavior, and avoid attaching to unrelated sidecars.
test_command: RED/GREEN: npm test --workspace=pmxtjs -- --runInBand sdks/typescript/tests/server-manager.test.ts; node sdks/cli/scripts/verify-server-manager.js; python3 -m pytest sdks/python/tests/test_server_manager.py; npm test --workspace=pmxt-core -- --runInBand core/test/server/pmxt-ensure-server.test.ts; core/bin/pmxt-ensure-server; python3 -c Hunch constructor smoke; npx tsc --project core/tsconfig.json --noEmit; npm test --workspace=pmxt-core -- --runInBand
observed_behavior: Retest caught lockless server-manager drift across TypeScript SDK, CLI, and Python SDK, then a current launcher drift where pmxt-ensure-server preserved an unhealthy existing lock and timed out instead of waiting for the replacement sidecar lock. After fixes, focused launcher red/green passed, real launcher exited 0 outside sandbox, Python constructor smoke passed, core typecheck passed, and full core suite passed 731 tests with 3 skipped.
notes: Server managers now require the current HOME lock port before health/status probes, Python wait-for-health waits for the new launcher's lock, and pmxt-ensure-server removes the matching unhealthy lock before spawning a replacement sidecar.
